entry.title: Re: New or not to the SAP Community, share your story!
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

My name is Vasudeva Raju, and I am pleased to join the SAP Community. I began my journey with SAP SuccessFactors in 2021 and have since been working with the People Analytics and Employee Central modules. I look forward to engaging with fellow professionals, expanding my knowledge, and contributing to the community.

entry.title: Re: Migrate SAP Fiori projects from SAP Web IDE to SAP Business Application Studio
entry.content:
Thanks for the helpful blog.

I have a related question: would this migration approach also apply to SAPUI5 applications that were originally developed using older tools (for example Eclipse/Web IDE-era projects) and are currently stored in the ABAP repository (SE80 as BSP)?

In some cases, these apps are downloaded using /UI5/UI5_REPOSITORY_LOAD (or similar transactions) and then manually uploaded into SAP Business Application Studio via drag-and-drop.

Would such scenarios also be supported by the SAP Fiori tools migration workflow described here, or would a different approach be recommended? 

Thanks,

Kenzi Sibuma

entry.title: Re: New Pipeline Concept features: integrated runtimes and supporting landscape stages
entry.content:
@mohankumar_mari3174f0 sorry for delay, somehow your comment slipped through.

Actually my whole blog series at https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/introducing-the-new-pipeline-concept-in-cloud-integration/ba-p/13639651 should help you to get familiar with the pipeline for Cloud Integration, it covers Pipeline features, not only related to migration.

Furthermore, I would like to point you to the accelerator tool from the community at https://community.sap.com/t5/integration-blog-posts/partner-directory-accelerator-tool-for-pipeline-concept/ba-p/14095019, you can use this to create the Partner Directory objects from scratch, for creating the scenario-specific flows you can use the templates that we ship in the templates package.

And also look at this blog post which guides you how to setup a very simple scenario based on a sample scenario that we provide on github, https://community.sap.com/t5/integration-blog-posts/how-to-get-started-with-pipeline-for-cloud-integration/ba-p/14325267

Alex

entry.title: Configure SAP Build Work Zone with SAP Logistics Management Content Package
entry.content:
Configure SAP Build Work Zone with SAP Logistics Management Content Package

Introduction

SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition, serves as a centralized digital workplace that aggregates business applications, processes, and information into a unified user experience. For SAP Logistics Management users, integrating the Content Package with SAP Build Work Zone enables streamlined access to logistics operations, real-time insights through integration cards, and personalized user preferences—all within a single, intuitive interface.

This guide walks you through configuring SAP Build Work Zone with the SAP Logistics Management Content Package, enabling features such as predefined integration cards, user preference plugins, and enhanced collaboration capabilities. The target audience includes SAP BTP administrators, logistics managers, and IT professionals responsible for system integration and user experience optimization.

Prerequisites

Before beginning the configuration, ensure the following requirements are met:

  • SAP Build Work Zone Subscription: Active subscription to SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition under the same SAP BTP subaccount as SAP Logistics Management
  • SAP Logistics Management Access: Valid subscription and access to SAP Logistics Management tenant
  • SAP BTP Cockpit Access: Administrator-level access to SAP BTP Cockpit for destination and role management
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services: Access to the Administration Console for configuring application dependencies
  • User Roles: Appropriate role collections assigned for content management and site administration
  • Content Package Files: Download the latest Content Package files from SAP Note 3605828, including:
    • LGMIntegrationCards_0.0.101.zip (Integration Cards)
    • UserPreferencePlugin (User Preferences Plugin)
    • LGMJoulePlugin_1.0.0.mtar (Joule Plugin)

What is SAP Build Work Zone?

SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition is a cloud-based digital workplace solution that provides a central entry point to SAP and non-SAP applications. It enables organizations to create personalized, role-based business sites that aggregate content from multiple sources, improving productivity and user engagement ,.

Key Capabilities:

  • Unified launchpad for accessing SAP applications
  • Integration of UI Integration Cards for real-time insights
  • Role-based content delivery and personalization
  • Spaces and pages for flexible content organization
  • Support for SAP Companion for in-app help

Benefits for SAP Logistics Management Users:

  • Centralized access to logistics applications and data
  • Real-time visibility into warehouse operations, shipments, and inventory
  • Personalized dashboards with relevant KPIs and alerts
  • Seamless navigation between logistics processes
  • Enhanced collaboration through integrated communication tools

Integration Architecture: The integration follows a content exposure and consumption model where SAP Logistics Management acts as a content provider, exposing apps, roles, and integration cards to SAP Build Work Zone through standardized APIs and content packages ,.

What is the SAP Logistics Management Content Package?

The SAP Logistics Management Content Package is a pre-configured bundle of business content designed specifically for logistics operations. It includes integration cards, user preference plugins, and application tiles that provide immediate value upon deployment.

Key Components:

  • Integration Cards: Predefined cards displaying essential logistics updates such as warehouse status, shipment tracking, and inventory alerts
  • User Preference Plugin: Allows users to set default preferences like default warehouse, enhancing personalization
  • Joule Plugin: Enables AI-powered assistance within SAP Build Work Zone for logistics tasks,
  • Roles and Permissions: Pre-configured role collections for logistics users
  • Spaces and Pages: Organized content structure for logistics workflows

Business Value:

  • Accelerated time-to-value with ready-to-use logistics content
  • Consistent user experience across logistics processes
  • Reduced configuration effort through pre-built components
  • Enhanced decision-making with real-time logistics insights

Configuration Steps

Step 1: Configure Integration Between SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Logistics Management

1.1 Define Application Dependency in SAP Cloud Identity Services

Navigate to the Administration Console for SAP Cloud Identity Services and configure the integration between SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Logistics Management:

  • Access Bundled Applications for SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition
  • Create a new dependency with the following properties:

Property

Value

Dependency Name

`` (e.g., LGM_Backend_Access)

Application

SAP Logistics Management ()()

API

backend-access

This dependency enables SAP Build Work Zone to consume SAP Logistics Management APIs for integration cards.

Step 2: Create Destinations in SAP BTP Cockpit

2.1 Create Integration Cards Destination

In the SAP BTP Cockpit, create a destination named LGM_Integration_Cards with the following configuration:

Property

Value

Name

LGM_Integration_Cards

Type

HTTP

URL

.lgm.cloud.sap/api (refer to Local Launchpad documentation)

Proxy Type

Internet

Authentication

NoAuthentication

Additional Properties

 

HTML5.DynamicDestination

true

HTML5.ForwardAuthToken

true

HTML5.IASDependencyName

`` (from Step 1.1)

This destination enables secure communication between SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Logistics Management for integration card data.

Step 3: Deploy the Content Package

3.1 Download Content Package Files

Download the latest Content Package files from SAP Note 3605828,

  • LGMIntegrationCards_0.0.101.zip
  • UserPreferencePlugin MTAR file
  • LGMJoulePlugin_1.0.0. mtar

3.2 Deploy User Preference Plugin

Using Cloud Foundry CLI, deploy the User Preference Plugin:

Copy code -->cf deploy

Example:

Copy code --> cf deploy mta_archives/UserPreferencePlugin_0.0.2.mtar

Step 4: Upload Content Package to SAP Build Work Zone

4.1 Access Channel Manager

  1. Access SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition
  2. Navigate to Channel Manager
  3. Select HTML5 Apps
  4. Click New Content Package

4.2 Upload Content Package

  1. Browse to the LGMIntegrationCards_0.0.101.zip file
  2. Set a Runtime Destination (use LGM_Integration_Cards created in Step 2)
  3. Click Upload
  4. Verify the upload status by clicking the Report link.

The report should confirm that the Content Package contains roles, apps, spaces, and pages.

Step 5: Update Content in SAP Build Work Zone

5.1 Refresh HTML5 Apps

  1. In Channel Manager, select HTML5 Apps
  2. Click Update Content to complete the deployment to SAP BTP

5.2 Add Content to Content Manager

  1. Navigate to Content Manager → Content Explorer → HTML5 Apps
  2. Locate User Preferences in the HTML5 Apps table
  3. Click Add to include it in your site content.

Step 6: Assign Roles and Permissions

6.1 Configure Role Assignments

  1. In Content Manager, search for the role (e.g., ContentPackageRole),
  2. Verify that apps and spaces are assigned to the role
  3. Click Edit on the role detail page
  4. Enable Assignment Status for User Preferences
  5. Click Save

6.2 Assign Role Collection to Users

  1. Navigate to SAP BTP Cockpit → Security → Users
  2. Select the target user
  3. Click Assign Role Collection
  4. Search for and assign ContentPackageRole

Step 7: Create and Configure the Business Site

7.1 Create a New Site

  1. Open SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition
  2. Navigate to Site Directory
  3. Click Create Site and provide a meaningful name

7.2 Configure Site Settings

  1. Go to Site Settings
  2. Click Edit
  3. In the Assignments area, assign ContentPackageRole to the site by clicking '+'
  4. Ensure Spaces and Pages - New Experience is selected
  5. Click Save

The new site experience provides a flexible grid-based layout with spaces and pages based on the Horizon visual theme.

Step 8: Test the Integration

8.1 Access the Business Site

  1. Click the Go to site icon to open the business site
  2. Verify that the SAP Logistics Management integration cards are visible on the home page

8.2 Validation Checklist

  • Integration cards display real-time logistics data
  • User preferences are accessible and functional
  • Navigation to SAP Logistics Management apps works correctly
  • Role-based content is properly filtered
  • Joule plugin (if deployed) is operational

Key Features Available After Configuration

Feature 1: Predefined Integration Cards

SAP Logistics Management provides several predefined integration cards that display essential logistics updates directly on the SAP Build Work Zone home page. These cards offer real-time visibility into:

  • Warehouse operations and capacity
  • Shipment status and tracking
  • Inventory levels and alerts
  • Order fulfillment metrics

Feature 2: User Preference Plugin

The User Preference Plugin allows users to set default preferences such as default warehouse, streamlining their daily workflows. Users can:

  • Configure personal logistics settings
  • Save frequently used parameters
  • Customize dashboard views
  • Set notification preferences

Feature 3: Joule AI Assistant

The Joule Plugin enables AI-powered assistance within SAP Build Work Zone for logistics tasks. Users can:

  • Ask natural language questions about logistics data
  • Receive intelligent recommendations
  • Automate routine logistics tasks
  • Access contextual help and guidance

Troubleshooting Common Issues

Issue 1: Content Package Not Visible

Possible Causes:

  • Incorrect destination configuration
  • Missing role assignments
  • Content not refreshed in Channel Manager

Resolution Steps:

  1. Verify the LGM_Integration_Cards destination is correctly configured with all required properties
  2. Ensure the dependency in SAP Cloud Identity Services is properly set up
  3. In Channel Manager, click Update Content to refresh HTML5 Apps
  4. Check that the role collection is assigned to both the site and the user

Issue 2: Authentication Errors

Possible Causes:

  • Incorrect IASDependencyName in destination
  • Missing ForwardAuthToken property
  • Trust configuration issues

Resolution Steps:

  1. Verify HTML5.IASDependencyName matches the dependency name created in SAP Cloud Identity Services
  2. Ensure HTML5.ForwardAuthToken is set to true
  3. Check trust configuration between SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Logistics Management
  4. Validate user authentication in SAP Cloud Identity Services

Issue 3: Integration Cards Not Loading Data

Possible Causes:

  • Incorrect destination URL
  • Network connectivity issues
  • Missing backend permissions

Resolution Steps:

  1. Verify the destination URL format:  ..lgm.cloud.sap/api
  2. Test connectivity from SAP BTP to SAP Logistics Management
  3. Ensure the user has appropriate authorizations in SAP Logistics Management
  4. Check SAP Logistics Management system logs for API errors

Best Practices

Regular Content Package Updates

  • Monitor SAP Note 3605828 for new Content Package versions
  • Test updates in a non-production environment before deploying to production
  • Communicate changes to end users before updates

User Training Recommendations

  • Provide role-specific training on integration card usage
  • Create user guides for setting preferences
  • Conduct workshops on leveraging Joule AI assistants

Performance Optimization

  • Limit the number of integration cards per page to improve load times
  • Use caching strategies for frequently accessed data
  • Monitor system resource consumption

Security Considerations

  • Regularly review and update role assignments
  • Implement least privilege access principles
  • Enable audit logging for sensitive logistics data
  • Maintain clickjacking protection allowlists

Additional Resources

  • SAP Help Portal: SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition | SAP Help Portal | SAP Help Portal
  • SAP Community: A Guide to Developing and Deploying Content Packag... - SAP Community
  • SAP Note 3605828: 3605828 - Configure the Build Work Zone with the Content Package of SAP Logistics Management - SAP for Me
  • GitHub Samples: build-workzone-integration/standard at main · SAP-samples/build-workzone-integration · GitHub
  • SAP Learning Journeys: SAP Work Zone | SAP Learning

Conclusion

Configuring SAP Build Work Zone with the SAP Logistics Management Content Package transforms the user experience by providing centralized access to logistics operations, real-time insights, and personalized workflows. By following this guide, you have successful:

  • Established secure integration between SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Logistics Management
  • Deployed predefined integration cards for logistics visibility
  • Enabled user preference customization for enhanced productivity
  • Configured role-based access to logistics content

entry.title: Re: Display CIM Approval Comment in S/4HANA Public Cloud...
entry.content:
Hello @Phil_P 

Thank you for your question.

Your scenario is fundamentally CIM to S4H Cloud integration, and CIM is a BTP application.

That means any approach to move an approver comment from the CIM workflow into the S4H Public Cloud workflow would be handled through integration and extensibility around CIM, not purely inside S4H Public Cloud workflow configuration.

See Integration with SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management for more details.

I suggest you raise your question to the BTP Community.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: New Pipeline Concept features: integrated runtimes and supporting landscape stages
entry.content:
@BHerbold thanks for your feedback, we have just published a new version of the packages which is currently in review before getting really public, so we plan to implement your fix for the version next to this version, we didn't like to put this into the coming version on the fly since this requires intensive testing in order to avoid that we break any other scenario.

Alex

entry.content:
Thank you for the answer @lesleyr ! 🙂 Bonuses and other pay components reflected in the amounts can have a frequency other than 12M or ANN (which is typically the case) in the pay structure which I use. 

I have pay components in amounts (which are included in Comp 3 Ration) are with frequencies such as Monthly 13, 14, and so on.

However, I found a solution yesterday: I created a custom column where I used the following setup and this columns helps me to make further calculation that I need

entry.title: Re: Tweaks for RAP BO events addressing filtering (before send) and too small payload (callbacks nee
entry.content:
Hi @zajde1jc 

Could be multiple things. First off I'd check the event monitoring /n/IWXBE/EVENT_MONITOR. If it's not there, your outbound channel configuration to your AEM isn't done proper for your custom event. 

Also, did you create an event binding for your custom event, for you did not mention that? Talking about 2.13 and 2.1.4 of my blog.

In AEM, check the path to the event you subscribed to is correct. Note how different standard vs custom is.

These are what I can think of "from afar". Otherwise, you need some more troubleshooting in your system. Debugging etc.

entry.title: NEW! - Enterprise management layer for SAP S/4HANA - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025 FSP00
entry.content:
On Monday March 2nd, 2026, the enterprise management layer team has just released its latest template system based on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025 FSP00.

The enterprise management layer is not to be confused with model company for SAP Business Suite.

  • The focus of the enterprise management layer template is large multinational organizations operating in many countries around the world. It provides the foundation of your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition by covering all Line of Business. It is by design industry agnostic, so that it can easily be used in all the industries covered by SAP S/4HANA.
  • Model company for SAP Business Suite focuses on the industry-specific content and is available for Germany and the United States.

Some of the Model company for SAP Business Suite is compatible and can be applied on top of the enterprise management layer.

The new release of the enterprise management layer is based on a selected number of scope items (220+) from SAP Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and specific content only available in the enterprise management layer. It covers up to 49 countries; 43 available through SAP Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and 6 only available through the content built by the enterprise management layer team. The system is delivered with 25 languages and is fully Fiori enabled.

The offering is split under in three groups as depicted below:

  • Standard Scope: activation of SAP Best Practices scope items that do not require a specific SAP license other than the core SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • Scope Options: activation of SAP Best Practices scope items that require specific SAP license
  • Service Options: activation of content only available in the enterprise management layer

Compared to the previous versions of the enterprise management layer, besides the fact that the content deployed by the activation of SAP Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition is based on the updated 2025 FPS00 content, the following specifics shall be mentioned.

New scope items:

  • Treasury and Risk Management (Optional scope):
    • TRM12 - Master Data: Treasury Management*
  • Order and Contract Management (Standard scope)
    • 3TD - Internal Commissions Settlement for Sales (replaces OTC30)
  • Service Operations & Processes (Standard scope)
    • 7GB - In-House Service (mainly description change)
  • Extended Warehouse Management (Embedded) (Optional scope)
    • 7VC - Synchronous Goods Receipts in EWM - External Procurement
    • 7VD - Material Replenishment with Kanban – Stock Transfer within EWM 
    • 7GP - Basic TM/EWM – Inbound Advanced Shipping and Receiving 
    • 7GQ - Basic TM/EWM – Outbound Advanced Shipping and Receiving
    • WM05D - Cross-Plant Stock Transfer with EWM in both Plants with Different Serial Number Profiles*
  • Advanced Shipping & Receiving (Optional scope)
    • 7VE - Advanced TM-EWM – Inbound Advanced Shipping and Receiving
    • 7VF - Advanced TM-EWM - Outbound Advanced Shipping and Receiving
  • Advanced Variant Configuration (Optional scope)
    • 7KW - Customer Returns for Material with Variant Configuration
  • Product Compliance (Optional scope)
    • 7DO Product Compliance Document Authoring
  • Embedded Production Planning and Detailed Scheduling (ePP/DS) (Optional scope)
    • 7UV Make – to – Stock Production - Discrete Manufacturing
    • 7V9 Make – to - Order Production  -  Discrete Manufacturing

* enterprise management specific

Removed scope items:

  • Order and Contract Management
    • 2QS - Analytics for Sales - Central Stock with Returns
    • OTC30 - Internal Commissions Settlement for Sales (replaced by 3TD)* 
  • Extended Warehouse Management (Embedded) 
    • WM01 - Synchronous Goods Receipts in EWM  For External Procurement (replaced by 7VC)*
    • WM02 - Synchronous Goods Receipts in EWM for Kanban (replaced by 7VD)*

* enterprise management specific

Updated Localization content:

CountryFeatureDescription
ATAnnual Financial StatementOBSOLETE: Use the Annual Financial Statement App to prepare an XML file that contains the annual financial statements of your company that you submit to the Austrian commercial register. You submit the data using the ERV-JAb data service (version 03.31).
ATAnnual Financial StatementNew report supporting ERV JAb 4.0 is available.
BEAnnual Sales ListingUPDATED: The Report has been adjusted so that the Representative information is optional and only populated in the XML, if the related Reporting Parameters are maintained.
BEPayment formatsThe following Payment Formats are available BE_CGI_XML_CT_V9 and BE_CGI_XML_DD_V8.
CATax Jurisdiction Codes in Document and Reporting ComplianceIt is possible to select more than one Tax Jurisdiction Code as part of the selection criteria of reports Canada QST Return, Canada PST British Columbia Return, Canada PST Saskatchewan Return, and Canada PST Manitoba Return.
CHProcessing Incoming Payments with LSV+ ReferenceReferred to the user-exit and solution required to populate the LSV+ Reference if required and how to use it in incoming payment processing.
CHCash Flow StatementYou can generate a Cash Flow Statement report using the Indirect Method (also known as C. Tableau des flux de trésorerie).
CLeBoleta additional fieldsThe following fields are populated in the XML:
• CorreoRecep - refers to the re-ceiver e-mail address
• TelefonoRecep - refers to the receiver telephone number
• MedioPago - refers to the pay-ment method
CNCash Flow StatementsOBSOLETE: Companies in China are required to generate a statement about the changes in their cash and cash equivalents in each period. You can prepare the cash flow statement in the required format as defined by law
CNCash Flow StatementNew features are available such as data preview, correction phase and manual adjustments.
CNBalance Sheet / Income StatementNew features are available such as being able to call the Display G/L Account Balances - Summary/Journal Entry Views app from within.
CNSplitting Cells with Multiple ValuesWith this feature, you can split cells with multiple values when exporting to a spreadsheet by choosing the option Split cells with multiple values.
CNAging Report (Accounts Receivable/Accounts Payable)UPDATED: A new selection criterion Reporting Currency is available and allows the user to select between Local Currency, Functional Currency, Global Currency, and First Freely-Defined Currency to display the amounts.
COUGPP ReportsThe UGPP reports allow you to generate your Trial Balance (report category CO_UGPP_TRIAL_BALANCE) and Auxiliary of Accounts (report category CO_UGPP_AUX_ACCT) and fulfill the statutory reporting requirements from the Unidad de Gestión Pensional y Parafiscales. The output is in a CSV format.
COAccount Balance Ledger ReportingThe Monthly Ledger (report category CO_ACCT_BAL_LEDGER) report enables you to generate a monthly balance of accounts and subaccounts. The output is in a CSV format.
CODIAN 350 Withholding Tax FormYou can generate the DIAN 350 Withholding Tax Report which reports on all types of Withholding Tax and generates the DIAN 350 PDF Form.
DEE-BilanzUPDATED: The E-Bilanz (electronic financial statement for Germany) is the required electronic tax filing in Germany. Updated with latest Taxonomy.
DEReduced Rate on User RightsWhen processing foreign supplier invoices related to User Rights a lower withholding tax rate should be applied for transactions amounting to up to EUR 10,000 in case there is a Double Tax Agreement between Germany and the country of the supplier
DEVAT Return FormatUPDATED: A new XSD format starting January 1st, 2026 is available.
ESEC Sales List Modelo 349 – Credit Memo ReferencingAll relevant credit memo must reference an original invoice. To reference credit memo, the Rebate Reprocessing Activity can be used.
ESCadastral ReferenceModelo 115 reports on withholding tax related to income from property renting or leasing. 
ESTax Register Book to SII through SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud EditionUPDATED: it is now possible to exchange the electronic documents using SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition
ESB2G invoices through SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud EditionUPDATED: it is now possible to exchange the B2G electronic invoices using SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition
ESVERI*FACTUYou can exchange electronic invoices through the VERI*FACTU system
FRPayment FormatsNew Payment Format FR_CGI_XML_CT_V9 available
GBPayment FormatsNew Payment Formats GB_CGI_XML_CT_V9 and GB_CGI_XML_DD_V8 are available
HRFiscalizationFiscalization refers to a set of measures implemented by companies in Croatia that provide an overview of cash transaction revenues. When a buyer pays the seller by cash, the probability of the seller evading tax by not declaring the cash payments is very high. To prevent this, the tax authority in Croatia, has instructed sellers to declare all their cash transactions.
HROverdue Receivable (OPZ-STAT-1)The Overdue Receivable report can be executed through Document and Reporting Compliance
HRWithholding Tax ReportingYou can report on suppliers’ transactions subject to Withholding Tax such as:
• Dividends and shares
• Services of foreign legal entities such as business consultancy, audit, tax consultancy, research markets etc.
• Interests other than the pre-scribed exemptions in accord-ance with the provisions of arti-cle 31 of the Corporate Income Tax Act
• Payment of copyright and other intellectual property rights
• Service of companies in other countries
HRB2B eInvoicingFrom January 1st, 2026, all B2B invoices must be exchanged electronically. The system now generates electronic documents for B2B transactions.
You can also generate electronic docu-ments for customer payments.
HUMonthly VAT Declaration Format (2025)UPDATED: New chapter about VAT Return Form 2665A version 2, and the latest requirements in terms of Reverse Charge Tax for some specific products
IDBPPU Withholding Tax Report The BPPU Withholding Tax Report is known locally as the Bukti Potong Pajak Unifikasi and generates an XML file with the related Withholding Tax levies.
IDBPNR Withholding Tax Report for Foreign VendorsThe BPNR Withholding Tax Report is known locally as the Bukti Potong Non-Residen and generates an XML file with the related Withholding Tax levies.
IDVAT OUT (CORETAX)The VAT OUT (Coretax) report enables you to generate a list of VAT items on sales to customers in an XML file. You can download the generated XML file and upload it to the Coretax system.
KREntertainment Expenses ReportProviding additional information about the prerequisites to run the entertainment expense report and the report output.
KRVAT ReportProviding additional information about the prerequisites for generating a successful VAT Report and the various mandatory/optional outputs supported.
KRMonthly Submission of Withholding Tax ReportsIt is now possible to submit the Withholding Tax reports for Business and Other Income monthly.
LUSAF-TArticle 70 of the Luxembourg VAT law and Administrative Circular nº 742, published in the Mémorial A-206 (Official Journal of the Grand Duchy of Luxembourg), effectively amending the VAT Law requirements determines that all businesses using electronic accounting systems are required to provide their bookkeeping data in a legible and intelligible electronic format, upon request by the Luxembourg Tax Authorities, in the form of the FAIA file (Fichier d'Audit Informatisé AED).
UPDATED: Generation of Periodic VAT Return through Document and Reporting Compliance
LUNew Payment FormatsThe following Payment Formats are available LU_CGI_XML_CT_V9 and LU_CGI_XML_DD_V8.
LUFixed Assets Appendix of Annual VAT ReturnThis report enables you to generate an XML file with fixed assets information to be submitted together with the Annual VAT Return report to the tax authorities through the eCDF platform.
MXWithholding Tax DeclarationOBSOLETE: This report is now obsolete and replaced by the electronic submission of Withholding Tax Certificates (see OSS note 3361404 - Sunset of Mexico Statutory Report for Withholding Tax reporting in DRC)
NLFinancial Audit FileUPDATED: The Financial Audit File is a standard for creating a xml file containing relevant data in Financial Accounting for the Netherlands. The file contains master data, opening balances and transactions that were processed during a certain period. The Financial Audit File is used by the Dutch tax authorities and approved accountants to perform audits of the financial administration of Dutch companies.
The file version available is 4.0
NOGeneral Ledger Account Line Items, Customer Line Items and Supplier Line Items ReportsA new configuration parameter has been introduced to limit the number of line items for the PDF generation to avoid the issues in ADS Server
PLReverse Charge VAT Declaration (Form VAT-27 (2))OBSOLETE: In Poland, certain transactions are subject to reverse charging. This means that the obligation to settle the applicable VAT amount rests with the purchaser and not the ven-dor. The vendor issues an invoice for 0% VAT (in enterprise management layer Tax Codes A6 and A7 are available) and then reports the reverse charge to the tax authorities through a supplement to the standard periodic VAT declaration.
This supplement is the Reverse Charge VAT Declaration (form 27) provided to the authorities in XML format.
PLOfficial Document NumberYou are required by law to number the documents you post to your customers, for example, customer invoices or credit memos, following defined rules defined by the tax authorities. Each invoice successfully submitted to the KSeF (National System of e-Invoices) receives a unique identifier known as the KSeF Invoice ID, which is distinct from the invoice ID (number) issued by the sender.
PLSAF-T JPK_WBThe SAF-T Bank Statements report (PL_SAFT_WB) enables you to generate the report on bank account transactions in an XML file format. 
PLWithholding TaxAdded a chapter about Withholding Tax requirements and set up in enterprise management layer.
PLAlgorithm for KSeF Reference Numbers You can use algorithm 805 in your Posting Rules to clear open items based on the KSeF Reference Number
PTPurchase Account ProcedureUPDATED: If Purchase Account Management is active, which is the case for Portugal, additional postings are carried out during the posting of receipts of goods and invoices.
PTAsset ClassesUPDATED: Specific Asset Classes relevant to Portugal are available in enterprise management layer. 
PTQR Code UPDATED: A QR code and additional information such as the ACTUD number are printed on the billing documents generated from Sales and Distribution
PTAdditional Tax Box Structure Types for the Periodic VAT Return and Annexes ReportIf your main location is not the continent, but the Azores or Madeira, you can use new parameters to set your Tax Box Structures accordingly
ROAccount CardReport FIŞĂ DE CONT can now be reported through Document Reporting and Compliance
RODocument JournalReport REGISTRUL JURNAL can now be reported through Document Reporting and Compliance
ROCash JournalThe Romania Cash Journal report enables you to generate your Cash Journal (local name: REGISTRUL DE CASĂ).
ROGeneral Ledger with Corresponding AccountsReport CARTEA MARE can now be reported through Document Reporting and Compliance
SAVendor CertificatesUPDATED: Suppliers are required to maintain several certificates that are pre-requisites for doing business in the country. For example, all suppli-ers that have 100,000 Saudi Riyals in capital are legally required to register with the Commercial Register Office and are issued a commercial registration certificate with a Commercial Registration Number (CRN).
These certificates are only valid for a limited time and must be re-newed periodically.
Added new Business Partner Certificates functionality.
SAVAT Return FormatUPDATED: The Saudi Arabia VAT Return statutory report has been updated to generate a PDF output that is like the online form provided by GAZT.
If you have maintained the International Address Version in Arabic in your Company Code the address is populated in Arabic in the Arabic Output Form

TRNew Value Mapping UBL_DOC_TYPEA new value mapping UBL_DOC_TYPE is available for Delivery Notes
XXDisplay G/L Account Balances in Country/Region Specific FormatUPDATED: This App displays general ledger account balances for specified fiscal periods and in a format based on country/region -specific accounting practices.
From enterprise management layer based on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025, the App has been renamed.
XXLocal Chart of AccountsUPDATED: List of Local Chart of Accounts available in enterprise management layer and which Financial Statement Versions is using them
XXFinancial Statement VersionsUPDATED: List of Financial Statement Versions available in enterprise management layer.
XXFlexible Billing Document NumberingUPDATED: Reference to Flexible Billing Document Numbering on how to address country specific billing document numbering required in Spain and Portugal
XXOutgoing Payment MethodsUPDATED: list of outgoing payment methods per country including payment format
XXIncoming Payment MethodsUPDATED: list of incoming payment methods per country including payment format
XXSpecial features in Document Reporting ComplianceAdded a note about the need to activate Business Function GLO_DRC to activate the special features or not.
XXInternational Address VersionsReferring to the functionality of International Address Versions and how to activate them
XXCountry-specific fields in Manage Journal EntriesReferring to which Country specific fields are available in App Manage Journal Entries 
XXRun Statutory Reports App UpdatesVarious new features are available in the Run Statutory Reports App
XXManage Document and Reporting Compliance AppUPDATED: Update list of Fiori Apps and relevant SAP Business Roles

entry.title: Enterprise Data Replication Governance Between SAP SuccessFactors EC and SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Introduction: 

Resetting the Full Transmission Start Date (FTSD) for the SuccessFactors Employee Central (EC) to SAP S/4HANA replication process is a very critical step and should be done carefully. FTSD controls the starting point from which organizational and employee data is transmitted from SuccessFactors to S/4 HANA. Changing the FTSD date forces the system to reprocess the historical data and perform a full replication. This step can also be helpful when resolving synchronization issues, correcting data inconsistencies, or restarting replication after a major configuration change. In the normal operating conditions, replications jobs are run in delta mode, meaning that only the changes will be transmitted since the last successful run. However, resetting the FTSD date can be necessary when resolving synchronization issues, correcting data inconsistencies, or restarting replication after a major configuration change.

This blog/article describes required steps to: 

1. Update the FTSD for both Organizational and Employee replication. 

2. Removing pending replication requests from the staging table. 

3. Reset replication queries so the system performs the full data load rather than the delta. 

Employee replication

1. For Employee Data Go into following SPRO path: 

2. Double click on the entry that represents the template group created by you, in this example our template was EC to S4 Employee Replication: 

3. Change Date to desired Date.  This step will prompt you for a transport.

Org Data Replication

1. For Employee Data Go into following SPRO path:

2. Double click on the entry that represents the template group created by you, in this example our template was SF to S4 Org Replication: 

3. Change Date to desired Date.   This step will prompt you for a transport

DELETING PENDING REQUESTS FROM THE STAGING TABLE

Since we have changed the FTSD date, we most likely want to delete any pending requests from the staging tables. 

For Employee requests,

1. Go to tranaction ECPAO_EE_DELETION

2. Choose status 01, 02, 04, 05.   

3. Change date to the day after the current date to delete all pending requests.

For Org Requests,

Go to transaction SFIOM_VIEW_ORG_REQS

Select the requests and hit delete

RESETTING QUERIES

We also need to reset any queries so that full loads are done before the first-time replication is run.  These tables store the last time the queries were run and when there is something on the table it will do a delta load instead of a full load

For Org queries:

Go to transaction SFIOM_RESET_QRY_ADM. Select all and hit delete query

For Employee queries

Go to tcode ECPAO_RESET_QRY_ADM.  Select all and hit delete query

Conclusion

Completing the above steps ensures that the system processes a clean dataset and prevents conflicts caused by lingering or partially processed replication requests. While this can resolve the sync issues and ensures data consistency between SuccessFactors Employee Central and SAP S/4 HANA, it should only be executed when necessary.

Also, before performing this exercise in the production environment, it is always recommended to coordinate with HR, integration and system admin teams. It is also advisable to validate this approach in lower environments when possible.

entry.title: Building an MCP Server to Query SAP BTP Usage Data with AI Assistants
entry.content:
Building an MCP Server to Query SAP BTP Usage Data with AI Assistants

Tags: SAP BTP, MCP, Model Context Protocol, SAP Joule, Cloud Credits, Usage API, Cloud Foundry, XSUAA, Security

Introduction

Have you ever wanted to ask your AI assistant questions like "How many cloud credits do we have left?" or "Which services are consuming the most resources?" and get instant, accurate answers based on your actual SAP BTP consumption data?

With Joule Studio + AI (to help with the MCP server) + SAP BTP Resource Consumption / Usage Data Management API (providaded by SAP), you can make it happen.

I came up with this use case because it comes up very frequently during onboarding sessions.

In this post, I walk through how I built and deployed an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server—something that has become much easier to develop today with the help of AI—to expose SAP BTP resource consumption data as tools for AI assistants and query it from SAP Joule Studio using natural language.

What is the Model Context Protocol (MCP)?

The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open standard that enables AI assistants to securely access external tools and data sources. Instead of the AI having to guess or rely solely on its training data, MCP allows it to call specific tools that fetch real-time information from your systems.

Think of MCP as building blocks that extend what your AI assistant can do — in this case, querying live SAP BTP consumption data.

Architecture Overview

┌─────────────────┐     MCP (HTTP)      ┌──────────────────────┐     OAuth2      ┌──────────────────┐
│  SAP Joule      │ ──────────────────► │  MCP Server (CF App) │ ─────────────►  │  SAP BTP Usage   │
│  Studio         │   Bearer Token      │  Node.js / Express   │   via Dest.     │  API             │
└─────────────────┘                     └──────────────────────┘                 └──────────────────┘
        │                                         │
        │  OAuth2 (client_credentials)            │ validates JWT
        ▼                                         ▼
┌─────────────────┐                     ┌──────────────────────┐
│  BTP Destination│                     │  XSUAA Service       │
│  (MCP_UAS)      │                     │                      │
└─────────────────┘                     └──────────────────────┘

How it works: 1. SAP Joule Studio reads a BTP Destination with OAuth2 credentials 2. The Destination Service obtains a JWT token from XSUAA 3. Joule sends requests to the MCP server with the Bearer token 4. The MCP server validates the JWT and executes the requested tool 5. The tool fetches data from the SAP BTP Usage API via another Destination

Prerequisites

Before you begin, make sure you have:

Requirement Details

Node.jsVersion 18 or higher
Cloud Foundry CLIcf command-line tool installed and configured
SAP BTP AccountWith access to the Usage Data Management API
PermissionsAbility to create XSUAA and Destination service instances
SAP Joule StudioEnabled in your subaccount (for the AI integration)

API Reference

The MCP server consumes the SAP BTP Resource Consumption / Usage Data Management API. You can find the full API documentation here:

🔗SAP BTP Resource Consumption API on SAP Business Accelerator Hub

This API provides endpoints for: - Cloud credits balance and contract details - Monthly and daily usage reporting - Subaccount-level consumption breakdown - Service-specific usage metrics

Security Considerations

Security is a critical aspect of this implementation. Here's how we address it:

1. Authentication with XSUAA

All MCP endpoints (/mcp and /mcp/sse) are protected by XSUAA authentication. The server:

  • Extracts the Bearer token from the Authorization header
  • Validates the RS256 signature against the XSUAA public key
  • Verifies the issuer (iss) matches the configured XSUAA URL
  • Checks the audience (aud) contains the expected app name

2. OAuth2 Configuration

The xs-security.json defines three security scopes:

Scope Description

$XSAPPNAME.accessBasic access to MCP endpoints
$XSAPPNAME.readRead access to usage data
$XSAPPNAME.adminAdministrative operations

3. No Hardcoded Credentials

The server never stores API credentials in code. All sensitive data is:

  • Loaded from VCAP_SERVICES (Cloud Foundry environment)
  • Resolved at runtime via the BTP Destination Service
  • Fetched from service bindings automatically

Available MCP Tools

The server exposes six tools for querying BTP usage data:

Tool Description Example Question

sap_btp_get_cloud_creditsCloud credits balance, contract phases, expiry date"How many cloud credits do we have left?"
sap_btp_top_servicesTop N services by cost or usage"Which services are consuming the most credits?"
sap_btp_compare_monthsMonth-over-month consumption trends"Compare our consumption for the last 3 months"
sap_btp_check_overusageServices exceeding quota with PAYG costs"Which services exceeded their quota?"
sap_btp_new_servicesRecently enabled services"What new services did we enable this quarter?"
sap_btp_cost_summaryCost breakdown by service/subaccount/datacenter"Show costs grouped by subaccount"

Step-by-Step Deployment

Step 1: Clone the Repository

git clone https://github.com/sap-ef/sap-btp-usage-mcp-server.git
cd sap-btp-usage-mcp-server
npm install

🔗GitHub Repository: sap-btp-usage-mcp-server

Step 2: Create the XSUAA Service Instance

cf create-service xsuaa application sap-btp-usage-xsuaa -c xs-security.json

Create a service key to retrieve credentials:

cf create-service-key sap-btp-usage-xsuaa my-key
cf service-key sap-btp-usage-xsuaa my-key

Step 3: Create the Destination Service Instance

cf create-service destination lite sap-btp-usage-destination

Step 4: Configure the Usage API Destination

In the BTP Cockpit, navigate to Connectivity > Destinations and create:

Field Value

NameSAP_BTP_USAGE_API
TypeHTTP
URLhttps://uas-reporting.cfapps.<region>.hana.ondemand.com
AuthenticationOAuth2ClientCredentials
Client ID(from your UAS service key)
Client Secret(from your UAS service key)
Token Service URLhttps://<tenant>.authentication.<region>.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token

Note: Replace <region> with your BTP region (e.g., eu10, us10, br10).

Step 5: Build and Deploy

npm run build
cf push

Verify the deployment:

cf app sap-btp-usage-mcp-server
curl https://<your-app-url>/health
# Expected: {"status":"healthy","server":"sap-btp-usage-mcp-server"}

Connecting to SAP Joule Studio

After deploying the MCP server, you need to: 1. Create a BTP Destination so Joule can discover and authenticate against your server. 2. Create a Joule Agent in SAP Build Lobby that uses this MCP server as its toolkit.

Prerequisite: SAP Joule Studio must already be set up in your subaccount. If not, follow the Discovery Center mission first: https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/missiondetail/4643/

Step 1: Get Your Credentials

Before creating the destination, retrieve the service key credentials for your XSUAA instance:

cf service-key sap-btp-usage-xsuaa my-key

Note down the values for clientid, clientsecret, and url. You will also need the app URL from:

cf app sap-btp-usage-mcp-server | grep routes

Step 2: Create the Joule Studio Destination

In the BTP Cockpit of your Joule Studio subaccount, go to Connectivity → Destinations and create a new destination with these properties:

Field Value

NameMCP_UAS
TypeHTTP
URLhttps://<your-app-url>
Proxy TypeInternet
AuthenticationOAuth2ClientCredentials
Client ID(from service key: clientid)
Client Secret(from service key: clientsecret)
Token Service URL(from service key: url + /oauth/token)
Token Service URL TypeDedicated

Then click New Property and add:

Property Value

sap-joule-studio-mcp-servertrue

⚠️This additional property is required. Without it, Joule Studio will not discover or register your MCP server as a toolkit.

Click Save → Check Connection to verify.

Alternatively, import this JSON directly in the BTP Cockpit:

{
  "destination": {
    "Name": "MCP_UAS",
    "Type": "HTTP",
    "URL": "https://<your-app-url>",
    "ProxyType": "Internet",
    "Authentication": "OAuth2ClientCredentials",
    "clientId": "sb-sap-btp-usage-mcp-server!t<N>",
    "clientSecret": "<clientSecret from XSUAA service key>",
    "tokenServiceURL": "https://<tenant>.authentication.<region>.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token",
    "tokenServiceURLType": "Dedicated",
    "sap-joule-studio-mcp-server": "true"
  }
}

Step 3: Create a Project in SAP Build Lobby

With the destination in place, open SAP Build Lobby to create the agent project:

  1. Click Create → select Joule Agent and Skill.
  2. Enter a name for the project.
  3. Click Review -> Create to confirm.

     

Step 4: Create the Agent

Inside the new project:

  1. Click Create Agent.

  2. Fill in Name and Description:

    Field Value
    NameGA_BTP_Reporting
    DescriptionAn AI-powered assistant that connects to your SAP BTP account to provide real-time insights on cloud consumption. Ask questions like: "How many cloud credits do we have left?", "Which services consumed the most this month?", "Compare our spending over the last 3 months", "Are any services exceeding their quota?"

  3. Fill in Expertise and Instructions:



    Field Value
    ExpertiseExpert on SAP BTP Global Account Reporting
    InstructionsCopy the full text from the Agent Instructions Reference.

Step 5: Add the MCP Server to the Agent

  1. Click Add MCP Server.

  2. Fill in the server details and link the destination:

    Field Value
    NameMCP_UAS
    Description for AgentYour SAP BTP FinOps assistant. Ask questions like "How many credits do we have left?", "Which services cost the most?", or "Compare January vs February spending" and get instant answers based on real-time consumption data from your Global Account.
    DestinationSelect MCP_UAS (created in Step 2)

  3. Click Add Server, then Save.

  4.  Add a calculator tool to the agent:

    • Click Add Tool.
    • Select Calculator from the list of available tools.
    • Click Add to confirm.

    This allows the agent to perform calculations when analyzing cost data.

Step 6: Test the Agent

  1. Click Test to open the test panel.

  2. Ask a sample question, for example:

    "Which 5 services consumed the most credits this month?"

If the agent returns real data from your BTP account, the integration is working correctly. 🎉

Conclusion

Building an MCP server for SAP BTP usage data opens up powerful possibilities for AI-assisted cost management and monitoring. With just a few configuration steps — deploying to Cloud Foundry, creating two BTP Destinations, and configuring a Joule Agent in SAP Build Lobby — your team can start asking natural language questions about cloud consumption and get accurate, real-time answers.

Key Takeaways

  1. MCP enables AI tools — Extend your AI assistant's capabilities with real-time data from custom sources
  2. Security first — Always protect MCP endpoints with proper authentication (XSUAA + JWT validation)
  3. No hardcoded credentials — Use BTP Destinations and service bindings for secure credential management
  4. End-to-end with Joule Studio — A BTP Destination + a Joule Agent in SAP Build Lobby is all it takes to bring your MCP server into a conversational AI experience
  5. Reusable pattern — The same approach can be applied to expose any SAP API as an MCP toolkit for Joule

Related Resources

GitHub Repository

🔗https://github.com/sap-ef/sap-btp-usage-mcp-server

SAP BTP Usage API Documentation

🔗SAP BTP Resource Consumption API – SAP Business Accelerator Hub

Model Context Protocol

🔗MCP Official Documentation

SAP Joule Studio

🔗SAP Joule Documentation 🔗 Joule Studio Setup Mission – SAP Discovery Center

entry.title: Managing LMS for SME's
entry.content:
Dear Community,

While working on several implementations of SAP SuccessFactors Learning, our team has encountered a recurring design challenge related to external training management.

Many of the organizations we support — particularly smaller or mid-sized companies — rely heavily on external training providers and therefore do not maintain large internal training catalogues within the LMS. Instead, their main process revolves around employees requesting external training, followed by approval, participation, and evaluation.

In SuccessFactors Learning, however, most LMS functionality is built around items. When we use the “External Learning Request” process, we notice that certain functionalities typically linked to items are not available or become difficult to manage (for example surveys, retraining rules, and some reporting options).

One possible workaround would be to create an item for each requested training. In practice, though, many of these trainings are very specific and may only be attended by one or a few employees. Creating an item for every request could therefore lead to a large number of highly specific items in the catalogue, increasing maintenance effort and the risk of duplicate items over time.

We are therefore exploring alternative design approaches that balance the need for LMS functionality with the desire to avoid unnecessary catalogue growth.

I would be very interested to hear how others have addressed this in their implementations:

  • Do you typically create items for external trainings, even when they are one-off requests?
  • Have you implemented governance models or naming conventions to manage this?
  • Or have you adopted alternative approaches (e.g. generic items, custom processes, or integrations) to handle external training requests while still leveraging LMS functionality?

Any insights or practical experiences would be greatly appreciated.
Feel free to share your thoughts in the comments or reach out to me directly.

Thank you!

entry.title: How to Create Purchase Orders Using BAPI Including its Custom Fields
entry.content:
Introduction 


This blog demonstrates how to create a PO document using BAPI BAPI_PO_CREATE1, along with populating custom field values via the table/changing parameter EXTENSIONIN in BAPI.


Procedure 


We know that the EXTENSION parameter in BAPI is used to pass values of PO's custom fields; however, its structure, bapiparex, is so generic that we are unsure what values to pass as inputs, and we lack detailed information from BAPI's documentation.

Upon raising the SAP ticket, we got the solution to refer document of BAPI_CONTRACT_CREATE. Let's follow the steps below:

1. EXTENSION parameter values can be an XML-structured input if we have extended the PO via custom fields and a logic app.

2. It's Input values should be in the auxiliary structure ‘Custom Extension data’; the data can only be passed in an XML format. 

Custom Extension data structures:

    • Header - MMPUR_OA_BAPI_HEADER_EXT
    • Item - MMPUR_OA_BAPI_ITEM_EXT

3. To convert this structure into an XML format, we can use the class CL_CFD_BAPI_MAPPING and methods  IF_CFD_BAPI_MAPPING~MAP_TO_BAPIPAREX_SINGLE  (for header data)  and   IF_CFD_BAPI_MAPPING~MAP_TO_BAPIPAREX_MULTI (for item data).

4. Refer to the code snippet for header data:

DATA ls_extensionin             TYPE bapiparex.
DATA extensionin                TYPE TABLE OF bapiparex.
DATA ls_po_bapi_header_ext      TYPE mmpur_po_bapi_header_ext.
DATA ls_bapi_header_ext_data    TYPE mmpur_po_bapi_header_ext-data.
DATA ls_bapi_header_ext_datax   TYPE mmpur_po_bapi_header_ext-datax.
DATA lo_descr_struct            TYPE REF TO cl_abap_structdescr.
DATA lo_descr_structx           TYPE REF TO cl_abap_structdescr.
DATA lo_bapi_mapping            TYPE REF TO if_cfd_bapi_mapping.
FIELD-SYMBOLS: <fs_comp_data>  TYPE abap_compdescr,
               <fs_comp_datax> TYPE abap_compdescr,
               <fs_data>       TYPE any,
               <fs_datax>      TYPE any,
               <ls_data>       TYPE any.

CLEAR extensionin.
lo_descr_struct  ?= cl_abap_structdescr=>describe_by_data( ls_bapi_header_ext_data ).
lo_descr_structx ?= cl_abap_structdescr=>describe_by_data( ls_bapi_header_ext_datax ).

ls_bapi_header_ext_data-zz1_custom_pdh = 'value'.

LOOP AT  lo_descr_struct->components ASSIGNING <fs_comp_data>.
  ASSIGN COMPONENT <fs_comp_data>-name OF STRUCTURE ls_bapi_header_ext_data TO <fs_data>.
  IF <fs_data> IS ASSIGNED AND <fs_data> IS NOT INITIAL.
    ASSIGN COMPONENT <fs_comp_data>-name OF STRUCTURE ls_bapi_header_ext_datax TO <fs_datax>.
    IF <fs_datax> IS ASSIGNED.
      <fs_datax> = abap_true.
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.
ENDLOOP.

ls_po_bapi_header_ext = VALUE #( key    = lv_EBELN "PO value
                                 data   = ls_bapi_header_ext_data
                                 datax  = ls_bapi_header_ext_datax ).
TRY.
    lo_bapi_mapping = cl_cfd_bapi_mapping=>get_instance( ).
    lo_bapi_mapping->map_to_bapiparex_single(
      EXPORTING
        ir_source_structure = REF #( ls_po_bapi_header_ext )
      CHANGING
        ct_bapiparex        = extensionin ).
  CATCH cx_cfd_bapi_mapping. " Custom Fields: BAPI Mapping Exception
ENDTRY.

Note :  In S4 HANA 2022 FPS01 

We observe that CI_EKKODBX is not included in the structure EKKO_INCL_EEW_X (which is used for updating the change indicator for custom fields)

We are using the MMPUR_PO_BAPI_HEADER_EXT structure to update the PO header custom fields, which consist of EKKO_INCL_EEW_PS  (to update the actual data) and EKKO_INCL_EEW_X (to update the change indicator).


EKKO_INCL_EEW_PS has all the custom fields, which are enhanced via exit MM06E005 (EXIT_SAPMM06E_006, EXIT_SAPMM06E_008) and via Fiori app Custom fields and logic.
But fields that are enhanced via exit are missing in the structure EKKO_INCL_EEW_X because CI_EKKODBX is not included.

Without updating the change bar, we can't populate the values for these fields via BAPI_PO_CREATE1, extensionin.

To resolve this, we have implemented the SAP Note 3334975.


Result


Use this method to fill extensionin parameter in BAPI_PO_CREATE1,  to pass the values of the PO's custom field to create the Purchase order along with its custom fields.

entry.title: Recreate dunning notices after completing the dunning run

Hi everyone,

So, there is the following problem: A dunning run was executed and successfully completed. The reminders were generated and the dunning levels/dunning dates, etc. were set correctly.

The dunning notices were also all successfully created and sent to the customer.

However, there was an error in the forms, and we now need to run the reminder process again so that only the forms are recreated. We do NOT need to run a new reminder process, but rather run the same one again, or recreate the forms.

We do not use FI-CA. Therefore, the ‘Cancellation Run for Reminders’ app is NOT an option for us.

How can this be done?

Thank you und best regards,

Nathalie

entry.title: LGM Knowledge Snippet: When do Transportation Requests and Pickup / Delivery documents get linked
entry.content:
In warehouse-integrated shipping processes, one of the most critical coordination points is when Transportation Requests (TRs) connect to Pickup/Delivery Documents (PDDs). This connection determines when the team at check in and warehouse staff know exactly what to load, which truck to load it onto, and when that truck will arrive. But when does this link get established? The answer depends entirely on your shipping process configuration.

Why the Timing Matters

The TR-to-PDD link serves as the bridge between transport planning (what needs to ship) and warehouse execution (the physical loading operation). Get the timing wrong, and you either start warehouse work before you know the carrier details, or you delay warehouse operations waiting for planning decisions that could have happened earlier.

SAP Logistics Management handles this through configurable Processing Variants, which define when the link is made based on your specific shipping process needs.

Two Fundamental Linking Patterns

There are two primary timing patterns for establishing the TR-to-PDD connection:

  • Late Linking - The link is made after warehouse processing completes
  • Early Linking - The link is made before warehouse processing begins

Which pattern applies to your shipments depends on the Shipping Process of the Processing Variant used.

Late Linking: After Warehouse Completion

In late linking scenarios, the Transportation Request goes through warehouse processing (picking, packing, staging) before it's linked to a specific PDD. This pattern is used for:

  • Parcel Shipping Process - Parcels are packed first, then assigned to carrier pickups
  • LTL Shipping Process - Similar to parcel, the carrier picks up what has been finished

If no PDD matches, the TR enters an error state requiring manual resolution.

What Happens When No PDD is Found?

This is one of the most common scenarios requiring user intervention. When the PDD service cannot find a suitable PDD:

  • The TR pickup planning status changes to ERROR
  • The TR is flagged with Action Required (reason: ERROR)
  • The TR appears in the "Needs Attention" list in the Manage Transportation Requests app
  • The PDD reference remains empty (no link established)

To resolve this, you have to perform the following steps (as of today, might be simplified in future...)

  1. Create a suitable PDD manually - Either through the UI or by updating Pickup Schedules to generate the needed PDD OR
  2. Retry the assignment - Use the "Retry PDD Assignment" action, which resets the status to REQUESTED and attempts matching again

Once a suitable PDD exists, the retry typically succeeds and the link is established.

Early Linking: Before Warehouse Processing

In early linking scenarios, the Transportation Request is connected to a PDD before warehouse work begins. This pattern is used for:

  • Direct LTL Shipping Process - Carrier is determined early via tendering, PDD is created when carrier confirms

In these processes, the PDD creation happens at the Consignment level, not at the individual Transportation Request level. When the consignment is created and the carrier confirms, a PDD is automatically generated with all necessary Transportation Request items already linked.

For these processes, the TR pickup planning status is set to NOT_RELEVANT because late linking doesn't apply-the link is established early as part of the broader document creation flow.

The Role of Carrier Confirmation

A critical prerequisite for late linking is that the Transportation Request must have a carrier assigned and a confirmation by the carrier received before warehouse processing completes. With the confirmation a dedicated PDD is created for the Consignment. There is no relation to a pickup schedule in this scenario. There might be changes to the 1:1 link between the consignment and the respective PDD in future, stay tuned on this.

Configuration Summary: Which Pattern Do I Use?

Your Processing Variant's shipping process type determines the linking pattern:

Shipping ProcessLinking PatternWhen Link is Made

ParcelLate LinkingAfter warehouse packing completes
Network LTLLate LinkingAfter warehouse staging completes
Direct LTLEarly LinkingWhen carrier confirms (via tendering), before warehouse work
No ShippingNo LinkingPDDs not used (warehouse-only scenario)

Practical Implications for Users

Understanding the linking timing helps you troubleshoot common scenarios:

  • TR shows "ERROR" pickup planning status → Check if a suitable PDD exists for the carrier, location, and time. Use "Retry PDD Assignment" after creating or adjusting PDDs.
  • TR shows "NOT_STARTED" after warehouse work → Verify the TR has a carrier assigned. Assign carrier if missing.
  • TR shows "NOT_RELEVANT" → This is normal for Direct LTL and FTL processes where early linking applies.
  • Multiple TRs stuck in "REQUESTED" → The PDD service may be unavailable or experiencing high load. Check system health or retry later.

Why This Design?

This flexible timing model accommodates diverse real-world shipping processes:

  • Parcel shippers pack first, then schedule pickups based on what's ready
  • LTL shippers stage goods, then assign to regular carrier schedules
  • Direct LTL shippers negotiate carrier rates upfront, and get confirmation including pickup info for the given consignment

Related Topics

  • Processing Variants: The Idea Behind Configuration
  • Processing Variants Configuration - SAP Help Portal
  • Pickup Schedules - SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to create invoices from an invoicing p...
entry.content:
If you want a purely standard solution, the closest fit is service POs with recurring service entry sheets.  These can then trigger invoice creation (self-billing style). It still requires an “entry,” but it’s lighter than a GR and fits service/subscription scenarios better. 
If you want automation without manual SES, you will likely need a custom job or enhancement that generates the invoice directly from the PO each month. This is a common workaround in private edition until the recurring invoice functionality is delivered.

entry.title: Re: A customer requested to downgrade some set of users f...
entry.content:
Hello @ibenjamin13 

Thank you for your question.

You are correct, in Public Cloud, you do not use USMM or SLAW. The Core vs Advanced classification is driven by the Price Category of the delivered business catalogs that are attached to the business roles assigned to the user. If a user has at least one catalog that is marked as Advanced.

The provides you the possibility to:

  1. Downgrade a user from Advanced to Core by ensuring the user only has business roles and catalogs whose Price Category is Core. Not Advanced and not Add on.
  2. Identify which roles and catalogs are making the user Advanced using IAM Information System and the Business Catalogs and Maintain Business Roles apps, because they expose the Price Category.

Changing your commercial subscription quantities (for example reducing purchased Advanced user quantity) is a commercial change handled via your contract process, typically through your SAP account team, not an in-system transaction. Your system work is the authorisation cleanup so your user no longer consumes Advanced usage rights.

See KBA 2873275 and Creating and Customizing Business Roles for more insights.

Here is a quick guide:

Open your IAM Information System if your user has multiple roles and you want a single consolidated list of all Advanced catalogs across all assigned roles. (You may also use Maintain Business Users app)

Search for the User ID, display the list of assigned Business Roles and their Business Catalogs,

find any catalog with Advanced price category. Then in Maintain Business Roles app, open the role used by the user, in Business Catalogs, identify the catalog marked as advanced, remove those catalogs from the role, or split into two roles (Core role: Only Core catalogs & Advance role: keep Advanced catalogs for your few users who need them). Save and publish the role changes. Then reassign the corrected role to the user and verify that the user is now core.

 

Your Scenario 2 questions: Is there a tool to know which Fiori apps makes a user Advanced user?

Answer: Yes, you should use catalog price category, not app counting. A practical rule is, if the user has even one catalog whose Price Category is Advanced, the user is Advanced. A Core user has only Core catalogs in all assigned roles. So, you do not need to remove apps. You remove or redesign roles to eliminate Advanced catalogs.

See the following reference for more details

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2873275

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Story not loading for a single user of SAC
entry.content:
Hi @MadhuLodugu48,

for me this sounds like a browser issue I heard quite often lately from my clients, due to some security/tracking prevention changes on browser side.

A good solution for this is described in SAP note 3216225. Short version of the solution (exemplary for Edge, Chrome is quite similar): 

  1. Open MS Edge settings
  2. Go to "Privacy, search and services"
  3. In Tracking prevention, click on Exceptions
  4. Click on Add a site button
  5. add [*.]hana.ondemand.com and [*.]cloud.sap 

Hope that helps!

Best regards,
Denis

entry.title: Re: Can you share an xsuaa service accross different spac...
entry.content: From my understanding, an XSUAA service instance cannot be shared across spaces or subaccounts in SAP BTP Cloud Foundry, so a JWT issued in Space A will not be valid for applications in Spaces B or C that use different XSUAA instances. In my scenario, the practical approach would be to configure each backend with its own XSUAA and use OAuth 2.0 token exchange (for example via Destination Service with OAuth2UserTokenExchange) so that the application in Space A can exchange its user token for a token issued by the target backend’s XSUAA before calling it. This way, each backend receives a token from the XSUAA instance it trusts while still allowing secure communication between applications across different spaces or subaccounts.

entry.title: Re: Auto create Ship to party while creating the sales order
entry.content:
Let say if two different Orders come with same Ship to Party, however there is little difference in Ship to Party address.  Here you will end up with 2 (or possibly) more Ship to Party Master records in your system. 

Looking at Master Data Governance principles, this is never a good idea. 

Master Data and Transactional Data should be separate processes and as mentioned earlier look at the option of using One time Customer Process. 

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: ABAP naming convention cheat sheet (layer)
entry.content:
Download the attached XML file (XML Word 2003), edit it via a text editor and add the below two lines at the top, open the file via Microsoft Word, and adapt to the names you use in your project.

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<?mso-application progid="Word.Document"?>
